preview_text:
The model rates Colombia as a strong favorite at 76% to win, leaving DR Congo just 4% win probability and a 20% draw chance. This represents a substantial gap between the two sides in the model's assessment.

The outcome distribution reflects a match tilted decisively toward Colombia, yet not a foregone conclusion. A draw carries meaningful probability—one in five—suggesting competitive balance in the middle scenarios. DR Congo's low win probability indicates the model sees limited realistic paths to an upset, though the 20-point gap between a Colombian victory and a draw means the match remains open to non-decisive results.

The model and sportsbook consensus diverge notably. Books assign Colombia 64%, some 12 percentage points lower than the model, and give DR Congo nearly 13% win probability versus the model's 4%. The gap is substantial enough to flag a genuine difference in assessment. Polymarket prices show an entirely flat 33% three-way split, reflecting retail betting sentiment that differs markedly from both the model and the sportsbooks. The dashboard makes no claim about which source is correct.
